summary: Backend tested for Técnicos Ya marketplace. 29/29 tests PASS after fixing a critical MongoDB ObjectId vs string mismatch that was breaking GET /api/auth/me, GET /api/technicians/profile/{user_id}, GET /api/reviews/user/{user_id}, and all visit/application accept/confirm flows. Covered: auth (register/login/me, case-insensitive email, duplicate, short password), categories, technician search/profile/update (with role auth), service requests (client-only creation, technician 403), applications (technician-only with membership check), reviews privacy rules (client↔technician cross-view allowed, same-role denied with 403, self-view allowed), pending reviews, and price calculation (base 9990 ≤6km, +1000/km after 6km, exactly 6km).

minor.issue: Pydantic v1 class-based Config is deprecated in models.py (8 warnings). Migrate to ConfigDict.
passlib trapped error reading bcrypt version (cosmetic warning in backend logs, does not affect functionality).

Returned user object uses original-case email (e.g. 'TEST_User@x.com') while DB stores normalized lowercase. Login still works due to normalization but the registered email in response is inconsistent.
When called as a technician, returns all requests system-wide (no client_id filter). Consider scoping technicians to 'open' requests near them by default.
No role/identity validation — any authenticated user can create a visit doc with arbitrary client_id/technician_id values.
Does not verify the referenced service_request_id exists or that it is still 'open' before creating an application.

action_items: Review the to_oid helper added at the top of server.py and the patched find_one/update_one calls (auth/me, technician profile, service-request detail, application accept, visit confirm, review by user, pending reviews). All user/category/visit/application/service_request _id lookups now convert string IDs to ObjectId.
Consider unifying ID strategy: either store all _ids as string UUIDs (set _id at insert time) OR add a Pydantic alias layer. Current ObjectId/string mixing is fragile.
Add validation in POST /api/visits to enforce that the authenticated user is the client (or technician) being recorded.
POST /api/applications should verify the service_request exists and status == 'open'.
POST /api/auth/register should return the normalized lowercase email in the response payload (consistency with login).
Migrate Pydantic Config classes to ConfigDict to remove deprecation warnings.
Frontend was NOT tested per request scope.
critical_code_review_comments: ObjectId vs string mismatch was a systemic bug — fixed at 11 query sites in server.py.
models.py uses deprecated Pydantic v1 class Config; future Pydantic v3 will break.
No input validation that referenced parent entities (service_request, visit) exist when creating child entities.
POST /api/visits is wide-open — any authenticated user could insert arbitrary visit documents.
seed_data_creation: Tests register ephemeral TEST_*@example.com users on every run (not cleaned up). Existing cliente@test.com / tecnico@test.com seeded users were used.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 29 backend tests pass. ObjectId conversion helper `to_oid()` is defined at top of server.py and applied to all user/category/visit/application/service_request _id lookups. Frontend not yet tested. test_credentials.md is accurate (cliente@test.com / tecnico@test.com / test123).
rca_of_issue: Root cause: server.py used `str(result.inserted_id)` everywhere when returning IDs and embedding them in JWT, but queried back with raw strings against MongoDB's ObjectId-typed _id field. Pymongo does NOT auto-coerce strings to ObjectId on equality match, so every `find_one({"_id": some_str})` against an auto-generated _id returned None. Mitigation: added `to_oid(value)` helper that returns ObjectId(value) when valid, else the original value (preserves behavior for collections that legitimately use strings as _id). Applied at: get_me, search_technicians user-join, get_technician_profile (user lookup + review's from_user_id), service-request list (client + category join), get_service_request (+app technician), accept_application (+ service_request update), create_review (visit lookup + user rating update), get_user_reviews (+ from_user lookup), get_pending_reviews (other_user lookup), confirm_visit (visit fetch + update + service_request update).
